news.title: Harmony Infra Ventures to invest ₹550 crore in Ghaziabad housing project
news.para: Harmony Infra Ventures has acquired full ownership of The Horizon Residences, a ₹1,600 crore project in Indirapuram, Ghaziabad. The company plans to invest ₹550 crore in construction, with substructure work already in progress for the two G+33 towers. The project offers 264 residences, with prices starting from ₹5.99 crore.

news.title: Puravankara acquires 9.73-acre in Bengaluru; Arvind SmartSpaces to jointly develop 58.25 acre in Ahmedabad
news.para: Puravankara has acquired a 9.73-acre land parcel in North Bengaluru with a development potential of 0.89 million sq ft and an estimated GDV of ₹800 crore. In a separate development, Arvind SmartSpaces will develop a residential plotted project on Kerala-Nalsarovar Road in South Ahmedabad, a joint venture with an estimated top-line of ₹180 crore.

news.title: Areion seeks RBI approval for ₹936 crore Aviom India Housing Finance takeover
news.para: Areion Group is seeking Reserve Bank of India approval for its acquisition of Aviom India Housing Finance. The deal, valued at ₹936 crore, is undergoing rigorous review. The RBI will closely examine the source of funds and Areion Group's governance. This assessment ensures the acquisition is backed by legitimate capital.

news.title: Booming demand for office and retail spaces drives Kochi’s real estate growth
news.para: Kochi's real estate market is booming, with office space leasing surging 18% quarter-on-quarter, driven by demand for decentralized hubs. Flexible workspaces are leading the charge, securing 37% of leased areas. Simultaneously, retail leasing saw 42,000 sq ft in Q1 2026, with high streets dominating and Panampilly Nagar rentals rising 19% year-on-year.

news.title: Subhash Chandra's Delhi bungalow sold for ₹1,260 crore
news.para: Essel Group chairman Subhash Chandra is selling a prime bungalow in Lutyens' Delhi for ₹1,260 crore. This deal is one of the most expensive property transactions in India. The property was bought in 2015 for ₹304 crore. Deal formalities are expected to conclude by December 30, 2026. Other high-value deals are also underway in the exclusive Lutyens' Bungalow Zone.

news.title: Fight of Karnataka RERA for financial autonomy runs into government wall
news.para: Karnataka Real Estate Regulatory Authority faces a loss of control over its dedicated funds.  The state government now manages the authority's revenue, impacting its operational autonomy. This shift leads to delayed recruitment, slow complaint resolution, and infrastructure constraints.  The authority seeks urgent restoration of control to function independently.

news.title: Karnataka RERA orders builder to transfer assets, upholds homebuyers’ rights
news.para: Karnataka Real Estate Regulatory Authority has ordered a builder to transfer project assets to the allottees' association. This includes land khata, utility meters, funds, and pending amenities. The authority also mandated the restoration of power supply to a complainant's apartment. The order aims to strengthen builder accountability towards residents.

news.title: Pune-based builder booked for cheating flat buyers of ₹50 crore
news.para: A Pune-based builder and partners face a cheating case for allegedly defrauding over 150 homebuyers of Rs50 crore. Buyers are left with stalled construction and unfulfilled possession promises for flats in Wagholi, despite making substantial payments years ago. The builder's claims of extensions and new agreements have not led to resumed work.

news.title: Policy to rehabilitate slum dwellers in Delhi finalised: Home minister
news.para: The Delhi Slum and JJ Cluster Rehabilitation and Relocation Policy, 2026, has been finalized, set to benefit four lakh families. Union Home Minister Amit Shah directed the Delhi government to expedite tenders for public-private partnership rehabilitation projects, aiming for five per month. The policy fixes the eligibility cut-off date for JJ clusters as January 1, 2025.

news.title: US single-family housing starts drop 1.9% in May; imported inflation increases sharply
news.para: U.S. single-family homebuilding dropped to an eight-month low in May, driven by rising mortgage rates and material costs, impacting overall housing starts to a six-year low. This decline, coupled with scarce labor and lots, exacerbates the housing affordability crisis and suggests a continued drag on economic growth.

news.title: Delhi civic body revenue rises 17% to record ₹14,549 crore in 2025-26
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi achieved its highest-ever annual revenue of over Rs 14,500 crore in 2025-26. This marks a significant increase from the previous year. Property tax and transfer charges were the largest income sources. Revenue from advertisements and leasing also saw growth. The civic body continues to manage substantial liabilities.

news.title: NCR to get four greenfield cities under Regional Plan 2041
news.para: Four new cities, named 'Namo Cities', will be built in the National Capital Region. This is part of the Regional Plan 2041. The aim is to encourage balanced growth across Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, and Rajasthan. These cities will be selected through a challenge. Rs 5,000 crore will be allocated for their development.

news.title: JSW Realty eyes ₹1,600 crore loan from Tata Capital for office project in Mumbai
news.para: JSW Realty is nearing a significant deal to secure a Rs 1,600 crore loan from Tata Capital. This funding is earmarked for a new office development near Mumbai's Bandra business district. The five-year loan is expected to carry an interest rate between 9% and 11%. Discussions are ongoing, and the transaction is not yet finalized.

news.title: Kalki Koechlin sells apartment in Mumbai for ₹2.55 crore
news.para: Actress Kalki Koechlin has sold her Mumbai apartment in Varsova Kiran Co-operative Housing Society for ₹2.55 crore. Purchased in 2015 for ₹1.95 crore, the sale reflects an appreciation of approximately ₹60 lakh over a decade. The transaction, registered in April 2026, saw a stamp duty payment of ₹16.08 lakh.

news.title: Guwahati builders' union backs government's plan for satellite cities
news.para: The Assam Real Estate and Infrastructure Developers’ Association backs the state government's plan for satellite cities near Guwahati. This initiative aims to ease pressure on the main city and accommodate future population increases. Planned satellite cities with good infrastructure are crucial for orderly growth and economic opportunities. AREIDA emphasizes stakeholder involvement in the planning process for successful project execution.

news.title: From Krishna Curve to Krishna CONIQ: Redefining the future of urban retail destinations
news.para: Krishna United Group is transforming Mumbai's Linking Road into a lifestyle hub. Following the success of Krishna Curve, the company is launching Krishna CONIQ. This ambitious project integrates retail, dining, entertainment, and office spaces. It aims to create an immersive experience engaging all five senses. Krishna CONIQ represents the future of urban retail and lifestyle destinations in India.

news.title: Peak XV-backed HomeLane plans India IPO within two years to fuel expansion
news.para: HomeLane, a leading Indian interior design platform, is gearing up for an IPO within two years, aiming for expansion into new cities and adjacent home furnishing categories. The company, backed by prominent investors, is focused on achieving profitability by fiscal 2027. This strategic move aligns with the projected doubling of India's interior design market by 2030.

news.title: Delhi: Crackdown on illegal constructions ramps up; 217 properties razed, 237 sealed in June so far
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi has intensified its crackdown on illegal constructions. On Monday, 14 properties were demolished and 25 establishments sealed. This drive follows a hotel fire incident. Since then, 217 properties have been demolished and 237 sealed.

news.title: India’s REIT, InvIT AUM may double to ₹20 lakh crore by 2030: Report
news.para: Avendus Capital estimates that India’s REIT and InvIT market could attract additional investments of ₹11.6 lakh crore over the next five years. Domestic mutual funds, insurance companies and pension funds are expected to be among the key sources of capital.
